channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean lawmakers are deciding whether to impeach the president over his martial law bid
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean lawmakers on Saturday began meeting to vote on whether to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived attempt to impose martial law, as protests grew nationwide calling for his removal.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes continue in Gaza and neighbors close their borders with Syria
item.description: Palestinian officials say at least 29 people were killed, including four medical staff, when Israeli strikes pummeled the area around one of the last remaining hospitals in northern Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against 'Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: As part of an investigation into the ‘Ndrangheta mafia in the city of Brescia, Italian police have arrested 25 people including a nun known for her prison work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Friday annulled the first round of the country's presidential election, days after allegations emerged that Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outsider who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's governing party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, making impeachment more likely
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s governing party chief expressed support Friday for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law this week, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon’s impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Lebanon closes all its land border crossings with Syria except one
item.description: Lebanon is closing all its land border crossings with Syria except for a main one that links Beirut with the Syrian capital Damascus, the General Security Directorate said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will decide if Palestinian authorities can be sued in US over attacks in Middle East
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to settle a years-long legal dispute over whether Palestinian authorities can be sued in U.S. courts by Americans killed or wounded in terrorism attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Closely-watched international climate case in the Hague wraps up its first week of testimony
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A closely-watched international climate case that could yield guidance for governments around the world wrapped its first week of arguments before the top court of the United Nations in The Hague Friday.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey’s Erdogan encourages Islamist rebels to continue advances as Assad regime scrambles to survive
item.description: Amid a fast moving seizure of Syrian cities by a U.S.-classified Islamist terrorist movement, the power politics of the Mideast is rapidly changing quickly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin signs agreement offering Russia's security guarantees to ally Belarus
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin visited Belarus on Friday to sign a treaty offering security guarantees for Moscow’s closest ally, including the possible use of Russian nuclear weapons to help repel an aggression.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Under assault by gangs in Haiti, a Kenyan-led mission fights back
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — An armored vehicle carrying Kenyan police rumbles through Haiti's capital with a shattered window that a high-caliber bullet failed to penetrate, a permanent sign of violence the officers face almost daily in Port-au-Prince.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Ailing kids wait months for Israeli permission to leave Gaza for treatment. Some die in the meantime
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The 12-year-old Palestinian boy was lying in a hospital bed in central Gaza, wracked with leukemia, malnourished and whimpering in pain despite the morphine doctors were giving him, when Rosalia Bollen, a UNICEF official, said she saw him in late October.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where Turkey stands as opposition insurgents advance in neighboring Syria
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — As forces opposed to Syrian President Bashar Assad make swift and significant advances in Syria, Turkey is watching closely for any spillover.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia's LGBTQ+ community is living in fear following new laws and court rulings, activists say
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Gela Gogishvili and Haoyang Xu led a happy life as a gay couple in Russia, even as President Vladimir Putin’s government took an increasingly anti-LGBTQ+ stance.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: In the 6-hour mayhem of South Korea's martial law, a woman's act of resistance was a defining moment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In the groundswell of fury and defiance that erupted among South Koreans after their president declared martial law, curtailing the country's hard-won freedoms, it was perhaps the iconic moment.
item.dc:creator: YONG HO KIM and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean lawmakers support suspending president’s powers after short-lived martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers are calling for President Yoon Suk Yeol's constitutional powers to be stripped after his short-lived martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Foreign minister says in interview that Russia will use 'all means' to defend its interests
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s top diplomat said in an interview with former Fox News host Tucker Carlson released Friday that Moscow doesn't want a war with the United States but will use “all means” to defend its interests. Sergey Lavrov argued that while Russia and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: Thousands of people have fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El Salvador's president is triumphant after his bet on bitcoin comes true
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — As bitcoin reached historic highs, surpassing $100,000 for the first tim e, El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele was triumphant on Thursday about his big bet on the cryptocurrency.
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas official says Gaza ceasefire talks have resumed after weekslong hiatus
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — A Hamas official said Thursday that international mediators have resumed negotiating with the militant group and Israel over a ceasefire in Gaza, and that he was hopeful a deal to end the 14-month war was within reach.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus seeks to copy neighboring Russia’s repressive LGBTQ+ policies, activists say
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — As police raided the home of a gay couple in the Belarusian capital of Minsk and brutally beat them, the officers didn't hide that the crackdown was aligned to similar moves in neighboring Russia.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Husband leaps onto polar bear that lunged at wife in surprise attack: police
item.description: A man in Canada quickly defended his wife by jumping onto a polar bear that lunged toward her at the couple's home in rural Ontario, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. raises alarm over Russian interference in Romania’s election
item.description: The Biden administration and lawmakers in Congress are raising alarm over Russian interference in presidential elections in Romania, a NATO ally that borders Ukraine and the Black Sea.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Masked man arrested outside Swedish royal family residence with knife, handcuffs, tape: reports
item.description: A man acting suspiciously while armed outside the home of a Swedish royal family was arrested Wednesday evening, authorities told media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The world’s tallest snowman, measuring 122 feet, built by residents in Bethel, Maine
item.description: In 2008, the residents of Bethel, Maine, spent a month creating what is still to this day the tallest snowwoman that has ever been made, according to Guinness World Records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters want more US involvement on world stage despite isolationist talk, Ronald Reagan Institute survey finds
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Institute released its sixth annual National Defense Survey and found 'record high' support for U.S. involvement abroad despite a growing sense that isolationism could be on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French government collapses after no-confidence vote passes against Prime Minister Michel Barnier
item.description: Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to resign after the National Assembly passed a vote of no-confidence on Wednesday, effectively collapsing the current government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appoints former Middle East negotiator as hostage affairs envoy
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his selection of Adam Boehler to serve as special presidential envoy for hostage affairs in his second term. While the position holds the rank of an ambassador, it does not require Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC
